attacking_style: Thrives in opportunistic breakaways on mixed terrain (cobbles, gravel), typically commits early in stages. Struggles with timing in reduced sprint finishes, showing hesitation in final 200-400m.
team_tactics: Historically deployed as diesel domestique for tempo control and GC protection. When given freedom, prioritizes breakaway infiltration with strategic energy conservation, including refusal to cooperate with rivals.
terrain_preference: Gravel and cobbled sectors reward his diesel engine (8th Strade Bianche, won Tour cobble stage). Exposed in reduced bunch sprints where timing and pure speed matter—multiple near-misses from perfect position.
vulnerability: Critical tactical hesitation in winning positions: sat up with 33s at 3km (Giro 2023), marked instead of sprinting in final 400m (Amstel 2019), passive in finale despite strong position (Vuelta 2018).
key_patterns: Repeated tactical indecision in winning positions: gave up 33s lead, marked rivals instead of sprinting (3 instances)
Strategic non-cooperation in breakaways to conserve energy for finale sprint (Tour 2022 win confirms pattern)
Diesel engine suits one-day Classics terrain better than stage race finales after long efforts
Transitions from pure domestique role to breakaway specialist with age, team consensus respected in moves
form_trajectory: neutral: 2022 Tour win showed tactical learning, but 2023 Giro errors at age 36 suggest decision-making inconsistency persists despite experience
